$schema_note: Combined verified cross-KG joins for the Proto-OKN FRINK federation. Merges the A-I clusters with the spoke-okn geolocation crosswalks: cluster J (ZIP, on spoke-okn's full ~42,724-ZIP set) and cluster K (county/state FIPS, including spoke-okn's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub). Duplicates removed. Counts are exact COUNT(DISTINCT) over the named graphs on the verified_on date, EXCEPT cluster O (ufokn), whose verified_count is a SAMPLED lower bound (ufokn is too large for an exact full-graph count within the 120s endpoint limit) - see its verified_count_note. A consumer keyed by KG shortname should surface, for a given shortname, every entry where it appears as left_kg, right_kg, or bridge_kg. Clusters: (A) disease/phenotype - oard-kg is the MONDO/HP hub joining prokn, rdkg, nde and ubergraph; spoke-okn attaches via its 180 DOID Disease nodes (DIRECTLY to the DOID carriers prokn/biomarkerkg/nde, A7-A9, and through an ubergraph DOID->MONDO bridge to rdkg/oard-kg, A10-A11), (B) CAS chemical, (C) gene cluster - keyed across THREE id systems: Entrez/NCBI Gene (spoke-okn, spoke-genelab, rdkg, biobricks-aopwiki; in three IRI forms - see C_gene_cluster note - joined by bare-id rebuild; C4-C8), Ensembl (gene-expression-atlas-okn, pankgraph, spoke-okn's schema/ensembl crosswalk, AOP-Wiki; C1-C3, F1-F2), and HGNC (prokn, bridged to Entrez through wikidata P354->P351; C9), (D) SAWGraph S2 spatial hub, (E) toxicology/AOP CAS, (F) AOP-Wiki gene bridge, (G) UniProt protein, (H) soil-carbon spatial leaf, (I) manufacturing supply-chain, (J) spoke-okn ZIP geography, (K) spoke-okn county/state-FIPS geography. (L) county/state-FIPS attachments of geoconnex, scales and nikg to the SAWGraph spatial hub (spatialkg) and spoke-okn, each carrying the same KWG/FIPS county key under a different non-obvious encoding (geoconnex: geoconnex.us/ref/counties/{FIPS5} reference IRIs on the GNIS county predicate; scales: a NUMERIC hasIdbCounty literal with the leading zero dropped; nikg: a phila.gov-rewritten KWG sfWithin IRI). Cluster L SUPERSEDES three base-file verdicts: scales (was an island on the unpopulated fips:CountyCode field), nikg (was 'no geographic key at all'), and geoconnex (only non-joins were recorded - its S2-cell and SAWGraph PWS reference-IRI non-joins remain valid, but the county-FIPS key is a separate, working route to the hub). Four further KGs profiled for cluster L (maudekg, phaseskg, nasa-gesdisc-kg, wildlifekn) share no materialized join key and are recorded as islands / thin threads. (M) biobricks-mesh: the NLM MeSH vocabulary attaches to ubergraph on MeSH descriptor ids (oboInOwl:hasDbXref 'MESH:{ID}'), 9,463 matches dominated by MONDO disease (8,202), making it a MeSH-keyed member of the disease cluster A; biobricks-pubchem-annotations was profiled in the same pass and is an island (keyed solely on PubChem CID, which no federation KG materializes). Additionally biobricks-mesh has a second, DIRECT MeSH route (M2): spoke-okn MeSH-tags ~165 of its Disease and SDoH nodes via mesh_ids/mesh_list (HTTPS mesh IRIs vs biobricks-mesh's HTTP), uniquely reaching spoke-okn's social-determinants layer that the MONDO route (M1) cannot. (N) fiokg (SAWGraph FRS KG): a full SAWGraph spatial leaf attaching to the spatialkg hub via owl:sameAs to native KWG IRIs - 681,045 S2 Level-13 cells (N1, the LARGEST S2 join in the federation) and 3,031 county-FIPS AdministrativeRegion_2 IRIs (N2; 3,032 to spoke-okn, N3) - and to the manufacturing cluster I on the bare 6-digit NAICS code (N4 sudokn 64/66, N5 securechainkg 301/301). fiokg and the sawgraph leaf OVERLAP directly in 4,712 Maine S2 cells (N6, leaf-to-leaf), useful because fiokg flags 188,057 EPA-PFAS-Facility instances against sawgraph's PFAS samples. fiokg attaches at the county + S2 levels only (no KWG state IRIs); 261 leading-zero-truncated admin IRIs are excluded (see known_non_joins). (O) ufokn (Urban Flooding OKN): a FIFTH SAWGraph S2-cell spatial leaf. ufokn encodes its S2 cells as BLANK NODES (typed schema:PropertyValue + its own kwg:S2Cell ontology class), not KWG resource IRIs, with the 64-bit S2 cell id held as a bare decimal string in schema:value - the IDENTICAL value spatialkg uses as the suffix of its s2.level13.{id} node IRI. Rebuilding that IRI joins ufokn DIRECTLY to spatialkg's S2Cell_Level13 nodes (O1, no value rewrite). Three type-bounded samples (1k/8k/20k cells) all show 100% containment of ufokn's level-13 cells in spatialkg, so this is a near-complete attachment whose exact cardinality is not enumerable within the endpoint limit (verified_count 7,670 is a sampled lower bound). Cluster O SUPERSEDES the base file's two ufokn non-join verdicts ('unprofiled-large' and 'S2 Level-13 = 0'): the 0 was a direct typed-NODE join against blank nodes (can never match) and the timeout was an unbounded scan of ufokn's ~11.7M S2 nodes. ufokn also carries finer S2 LEVEL-18 cells that nest inside the level-13 hub cells but do not attach to the (level-13) hub directly. (P) biobricks-pubchem-annotations PubChem CID -> spoke-okn: spoke-okn materializes the CID as oboInOwl:hasDbXref identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{n} on its ChemicalEntity nodes (762, exact 100% join after bare-CID normalization), SUPERSEDING the base file's biobricks-pubchem-annotations island verdict - it never checked spoke-okn's hasDbXref.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki stressor chemicals are https://identifiers.org/cas/ (https); the toxcast/ice/tox21 cluster is http://identifiers.org/cas/ (http). Rewrite at query time: BIND(IRI(REPLACE(STR(?cas),'https://identifiers.org/cas/','http://identifiers.org/cas/')) AS ?c2), then join on ?c2.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Chemical stressors of Adverse Outcome Pathways (AOP-Wiki) that are also assayed in ToxCast / Tox21 / ICE.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same https->http Ensembl rewrite as F1. GXA Ensembl genes are node IRIs (http://identifiers.org/ensembl/), so the rewritten AOP-Wiki id joins directly on GXA's subject.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Baseline tissue expression (GXA) for genes implicated as AOP key-event targets (AOP-Wiki).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Three UniProt IRI forms exist across the protein KGs. AOP-Wiki proteins are own-node IRIs https://identifiers.org/uniprot/{ACC}; ProKN proteins (up:Protein) are http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/{ACC}. Extract the bare accession (REPLACE(STR(?u),'^.*/','')) and rebuild the target form: IRI(CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/', ?acc)). Note: do NOT additionally constrain the ProKN side with 'a up:Protein' in the join (some shared accessions ride other ProKN classes); match on IRI existence.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Proteins acting as AOP molecular targets (AOP-Wiki) with their ProKN protein-centric annotations (domains, PTMs, marker genes, disease links).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Both sides are the ncbigene form; only https (aopwiki) vs http (rdkg) differs. Rewrite https->http (or strip to bare id and rebuild) and join. No numeric transform needed.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared between AOP-Wiki key-event targets and rdkg rare-disease genes.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: AOP key-event target genes (AOP-Wiki) that are also rare-disease genes (rdkg) - toxicology pathway meets rare-disease genetics.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki gene/key-event targets carry Entrez as skos:exactMatch -> https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id}. Strip to the bare id and rebuild spoke-genelab's http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id}. NOTE: a genuinely NEW connection - spoke-genelab is Entrez-only, so AOP-Wiki (joined to the gene cluster on Ensembl in F1/F2) could not reach spoke-genelab before; the Entrez route is what links them.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (AOP-Wiki carries ~1,480 ncbigene exactMatch ids).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Gene targets of AOP molecular initiating / key events (AOP-Wiki) that have spaceflight/omics evidence in spoke-genelab.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki gene targets are skos:exactMatch -> https://identifiers.org/ensembl/{ENSG} (https); the Ensembl cluster (spoke-okn, GXA, pankgraph; base file C-series) uses http://identifiers.org/ensembl/ (http). Rewrite https->http and join.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Gene targets of AOP molecular initiating / key events (AOP-Wiki) with their spoke-okn network/disease/compound associations.
ToxCast-screened chemicals (CAS) with Integrated Chemical Environment safety/curation data.

verified_crosswalks.right_predicate: spoke-okn/schema/mesh_list (-> biolink:Disease / DOID nodes) ; spoke-okn/schema/mesh_ids (-> SDoH nodes)
verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: http://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} (biobricks-mesh node IRI, HTTP) ; https://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} IRIs on spoke-okn mesh_ids / mesh_list (HTTPS)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: DIRECT join, no bridge - but watch the scheme mismatch: biobricks-mesh entity IRIs use http://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} while spoke-okn's mesh_ids / mesh_list objects use HTTPS (https://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID}), so a naive owl:sameAs / IRI-equality join returns 0. Normalize to the bare descriptor id on both sides: REPLACE(STR(?x),'^https?://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/','') (or rewrite http->https), via two per-graph DISTINCT sub-SELECTs intersected on ?id. spoke-okn exposes MeSH through TWO predicates (mesh_ids and mesh_list) - union both.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: A SECOND, DIRECT attachment for biobricks-mesh, complementary to M1 (the ubergraph route). spoke-okn MeSH-tags ~165 nodes: 159 biolink:Disease nodes via mesh_list (155 distinct MeSH) and 10 SDoH nodes via mesh_ids, all matching biobricks-mesh. All spoke-okn Disease nodes are DOID-identified, so the disease MeSH tags sit on the SAME entities the established DOID join reaches (see overlap_with_doid_join) - making M2's disease half a MeSH<->DOID map rather than new disease coverage. Its UNIQUE value is (a) that MeSH<->DOID correspondence on 159 shared nodes and (b) the 10 SDoH / social-determinants nodes (Social Vulnerability D000091482, Internet Access D000077230, Child Poverty D000091486, Medically Uninsured D017057, Premature Mortality D061213), which have no MONDO equivalent and so are invisible to both the DOID join and the M1 MeSH->MONDO route.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Map spoke-okn's social-determinants and disease nodes to MeSH (e.g. Social Vulnerability = MeSH D000091482) to pull MeSH definitions / tree placement from biobricks-mesh, or to connect spoke-okn disease nodes to MeSH-tagged concepts - reaching the SDoH layer that the MONDO route (M1) misses.
verified_crosswalks.overlap_with_doid_join: Checked against spoke-okn's established DOID disease join. spoke-okn has 180 biolink:Disease nodes, ALL of them DOID-identified (the node IRI is http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{n}); mesh_list hangs the MeSH id directly on 159 of those same DOID nodes (155 distinct MeSH). So for DISEASES the MeSH route and the DOID route reach the SAME entities - MeSH is a co-located annotation on the DOID nodes, not a different slice (0 MeSH-tagged diseases are non-DOID; 21 DOID disease nodes simply carry no MeSH). M2's disease half therefore adds no new spoke-okn disease entities beyond the DOID join, but it DOES materialize a verified MeSH<->DOID correspondence on those 159 shared nodes (e.g. D015212<->DOID:0050589 inflammatory bowel disease, D000071243<->DOID:0060478 Zika fever), usable as a MeSH->DOID->MONDO bridge. The genuinely unique reach is the 10 SDoH nodes (mesh_ids), which are not Disease/DOID and have no MONDO term, so neither the DOID join nor M1 touches them.

cross_route_validation.question: Do spoke-okn's MeSH<->DOID disease pairs (M2) agree with ubergraph's independent MeSH->MONDO xrefs (M1), once DOID is bridged to MONDO via ubergraph?
cross_route_validation.method: Took all 164 spoke (DOID, MeSH) disease pairs; mapped DOID->MONDO via ubergraph oboInOwl:hasDbXref 'DOID:{n}' (ubergraph holds 11,866 distinct DOID xrefs, and all 164 spoke DOIDs map), then compared the 'MESH:{id}' xref(s) on that same MONDO to spoke's MeSH. GOTCHA: matching a BIND-constructed string directly against the stored xref TERM returned 0 (literal datatype mismatch); the comparison only works when both sides are reduced to bare strings via STR()+REPLACE and intersected - same class of bug as the http/https issue in M2 and the cross-graph FILTER gotcha in K1/methodology_note.
cross_route_validation.interpretation: 72% (118/164) exact two-route corroboration. 19.5% (32/164) are pairs where ubergraph carries NO MeSH on the mapped MONDO - so M2 ADDS MeSH coverage ubergraph lacks, not a conflict. Only 8.5% (14/164) are true disagreements, and label inspection shows these are SEMANTICALLY ADJACENT, not flat errors: (a) organism-vs-disease axis - spoke tags the disease node with the pathogen's MeSH while ubergraph uses the disease term (chlamydia: D002689 'Chlamydia' [genus] vs D002690 'Chlamydia Infections'; pertussis: D001885 'Bordetella Infections' vs D014917 'Whooping Cough'; also diphtheria, syphilis); and (b) parent/child or near-synonym granularity (depression D003863 'Depression' vs D003866 'Depressive Disorder'; squamous cell D018307 'Neoplasms, Squamous Cell' vs D002294 'Carcinoma, Squamous Cell'; nasopharynx D009303 'Nasopharyngeal Neoplasms' vs D000077274 'Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma'; tuberculosis D014375 'Tuberculoma' vs D014376 'Tuberculosis'; plus hepatitis, gout, uterine fibroid, generalized epilepsy, coronary artery disease, viral infectious disease).
cross_route_validation.upshot: MeSH is a HIGH- but not perfect-confidence bridge key. Exact-ID joins are trustworthy (72% corroborated by two independent routes, zero flat errors among the disagreements), but MeSH<->disease-ontology mappings are APPROXIMATE near the leaves - the main hazard is the organism-vs-disease axis (infections), where MeSH and DOID/MONDO legitimately pick different terms. For a high-precision federated join, prefer two-route corroboration (M1 & M2 agreeing) as a filter, and treat single-route MeSH leaf mappings for infectious diseases with care.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: http://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} (biobricks-mesh node IRI) ; "MESH:{ID}" string literal on oboInOwl:hasDbXref (ubergraph)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: biobricks-mesh entities ARE their own MeSH descriptor IRIs http://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} (e.g. .../D001249 = Asthma, .../D000086382 = COVID-19); extract the bare id REPLACE(STR(?s),'^http://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/',''). ubergraph carries MeSH cross-refs as oboInOwl:hasDbXref STRING LITERALS of the form 'MESH:{ID}' on OBO terms; strip the prefix REPLACE(STR(?x),'^MESH:',''). Join the two bare-id strings via two independent DISTINCT sub-SELECTs (one scoped to each named graph) and intersect on the shared ?id - a cross-graph FILTER(STR(?a)=?b) returns 0 (same gotcha recorded for K1 / methodology_note).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS biobricks-mesh's attachment to the federation. biobricks-mesh is a standalone MeSH vocabulary keyed only by its own MeSH descriptor IRIs (it carries no outbound ontology xref of its own), so it reaches the federation through ubergraph, which cross-references 10,004 MeSH ids on OBO terms. 9,463 MeSH descriptors match - 8,202 of them MONDO disease terms - so via ubergraph's MONDO hub biobricks-mesh joins the entire disease cluster (A): oard-kg, rdkg, prokn and nde (which key disease on MONDO/HP) and spoke-okn (through the ubergraph DOID<->MONDO bridge, A10-A11). The match also reaches 1,051 UBERON anatomy and 83 CL cell-type terms. NOTE: MeSH chemical (D-category) descriptors pick up NO CHEBI xref on this predicate (0 CHEBI matches), so this is a disease/anatomy bridge, not a chemical one.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Take a MeSH descriptor (e.g. Asthma = MeSH D001249), resolve it to MONDO via ubergraph, and pull matching disease records across oard-kg / rdkg / prokn / nde - optionally expanding to MONDO subtypes first with ubergraph's subClassOf* closure.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same PubChem CID, two IRI forms - a direct IRI match returns 0. biobricks oa:hasTarget (also dc:subject) objects are http://rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/compound/CID{n}; spoke-okn's 798 biolink:ChemicalEntity nodes carry the CID as oboInOwl:hasDbXref objects http://identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{n}. Strip both to the bare numeric CID and join. Drive from spoke-okn's 762 CIDs (small side): BIND(REPLACE(STR(?x),'http://identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/','') AS ?num), then BIND(IRI(CONCAT('http://rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/compound/CID',?num)) AS ?bio) and probe biobricks ?ann oa:hasTarget ?bio (its 14.4M-target index makes the bound lookup fast).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: Exact COUNT(DISTINCT) on 2026-06-11. 762/762 = 100% of spoke-okn's pubchem.compound xref-bearing ChemicalEntity nodes have >=1 biobricks annotation. spoke-okn is the ONLY federation KG that materializes PubChem CID, so it is biobricks-pubchem-annotations' sole partner.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: What PubChem free-text annotations (toxicity, hazards, uses) exist for the chemicals SPOKE links to diseases and genes?
Tox21-screened chemicals (CAS) tied to Integrated Chemical Environment safety/curation data.

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: tox21 keys chemicals as its OWN node IRIs (http://identifiers.org/cas/), exactly the form toxcast/ice carry as the object of edam:has_identifier (base file B1). tox21 therefore joins the existing CAS cluster directly, no rewrite. tox21 carries 8947 distinct CAS-typed subjects total.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Tox21 high-throughput screening hits (CAS) for chemicals also covered by EPA ToxCast assays.

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: biomarkerkg keys disease on DOID, not MONDO; ProKN carries both and is the hinge into the MONDO side. To reach MONDO graphs, route DOID->MONDO through ubergraph oboInOwl:hasDbXref / skos:exactMatch.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Literature biomarkers for a disease (BiomarkerKG) cross-referenced to protein/marker-gene evidence (ProKN).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: DIRECT leaf-to-leaf join: both fiokg facilities and sawgraph PFAS samples/features carry their S2 Level-13 cell as an owl:sameAs object in the IDENTICAL KWG form http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id}, so bind the sawgraph owl:sameAs object and reuse the SAME variable as the fiokg owl:sameAs object (filter to the s2.level13 substring on at least one side). No rewrite. (Unlike the other SAWGraph leaves, which only meet THROUGH the hub, fiokg and sawgraph genuinely OVERLAP geographically because fiokg is national and sawgraph is Maine, so they co-occupy Maine cells.)
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: A rare, genuinely useful DIRECT leaf-to-leaf spatial join (most SAWGraph leaves only meet through the spatialkg hub). 4,712 S2 Level-13 cells carry BOTH a sawgraph PFAS sample/feature and a fiokg EPA FRS facility - i.e. PFAS observations and regulated facilities in the same ~1 km cell in Maine (sawgraph's footprint). This is especially pointed because fiokg flags 188,057 facilities as EPA-PFAS-Facility (industries likely to handle PFAS): the cell-level co-location lets you ask which PFAS detections sit near which PFAS-relevant facilities. For non-Maine analysis, both still roll up to the national hub via N1/D2.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: In Maine, find S2 cells where a sawgraph PFAS sample co-occurs with an EPA FRS facility (fiokg) - especially EPA-PFAS-Facility-typed ones - to relate PFAS detections to nearby potentially-PFAS-handling facilities.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: fiokg: extract the bare code from http://w3id.org/fio/v1/naics#NAICS-{6-digit} as in N4. securechainkg: the classifier IRI is http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/naics-{code}.0-inst (lowercase 'naics-', trailing '.0', '-inst' suffix); extract REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*/naics-([0-9]+)\.0-inst$','$1') - same as base-file I1. Direct IRI join is 0; intersect the two bare numeric codes via two DISTINCT sub-SELECTs.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ALL 301 of securechainkg's distinct NAICS codes are present in fiokg (complete containment of securechainkg's industry codes in fiokg's national all-sector facility set). fiokg therefore reaches securechainkg's software/hardware supply-chain participants and SUDOKN's manufacturers (N4) through the shared NAICS classification - the same industry-class join securechainkg and sudokn use between themselves (I1). Industry-class level, not individual instances.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Cross-reference the NAICS industries of SecureChain supply-chain participants and SUDOKN manufacturers with the EPA FRS facilities (fiokg) in those same industries, then locate those facilities on the spatial hub via fiokg's S2/county links.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: fiokg facilities carry their S2 Level-13 location cell as an owl:sameAs object in the native KWG resource form http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id} - the IDENTICAL form spatialkg materializes as its S2Cell_Level13 nodes (base-file D1/D2/H1), so the join is DIRECT with no rewrite: bind the owl:sameAs object in the fiokg GRAPH block and reuse the SAME variable as the subject in the spatialkg GRAPH block (do NOT use a cross-graph FILTER(STR(?a)=?b), which returns 0 - same gotcha as K1/methodology_note). Constrain the spatialkg side with 'a kwg:S2Cell_Level13' to scope to cells.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS fiokg's primary attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub, and the LARGEST S2 join in the federation: 681,045 of fiokg's 681,108 S2 cells are present in spatialkg (near-complete containment; the ~63 unmatched are AK/HI/PR/territory cells spatialkg does not load). fiokg is now a FOURTH S2-keyed leaf on the hub alongside sawgraph (Maine PFAS, 88,007; D2), hydrologykg (Illinois wells/streams, 106,138; D1) and sockg (multi-state soil carbon, 1,069; H1) - and by far the largest, because fiokg's EPA FRS facilities are national. Via this key every FRS facility rolls up to spatialkg's S2 grid and AdministrativeRegion hierarchy, and meets the other S2 leaves in any shared cell.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Place EPA FRS facilities (fiokg) on the national S2 grid (spatialkg), roll them up to county/state admin regions, and find which facilities share an S2 cell with PFAS samples (sawgraph) or hydrologic features (hydrologykg).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: fiokg facilities ALSO carry their county as an owl:sameAs object in the native KWG form http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS5} - the IDENTICAL form spatialkg uses for its AdministrativeRegion_2 county nodes, so the clean 5-digit entries join DIRECTLY with no rewrite (bind the owl:sameAs object and reuse the SAME variable as the spatialkg subject; constrain spatialkg with 'a kwg:AdministrativeRegion_2'). CAVEAT: filter fiokg's admin objects to the 5-digit suffix - FILTER(STRLEN(REPLACE(STR(?reg),'^.*administrativeRegion\.USA\.',''))=5) - because a minority of fiokg admin IRIs are leading-zero-truncated to 3 or 4 digits and do NOT match the zero-padded hub form (see known_non_joins).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: fiokg's county-level attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub. 3,031 of fiokg's 3,040 clean 5-digit county FIPS match a spatialkg AdministrativeRegion_2 county (spatialkg covers the 48 contiguous states + DC; the unmatched ~9 are AK/HI/PR/territory counties). ALL matches are county-level (AdministrativeRegion_2); fiokg carries NO KWG state (AdministrativeRegion_1) IRIs, so there is no state-level rollup key on this route - go up via spatialkg's admin-region hierarchy instead. This county key is the same one cluster K/L members (spoke-okn K1, ruralkg K2, sockg K3, geoconnex L1, scales L4, nikg L6) share, so fiokg meets all of them at the county level through the hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate EPA FRS facilities (fiokg) to the county, place those counties on the spatialkg admin-region hierarchy, and join to county-keyed members (e.g. SCALES federal-court activity, ruralkg rural-resilience indicators) for the same county.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Extract the bare 5-digit county FIPS from fiokg's KWG admin-region owl:sameAs object (REPLACE(STR(?reg),'^.*administrativeRegion\.USA\.','') with STRLEN=5), rebuild spoke-okn's node IRI BIND(IRI(CONCAT('https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/spoke-okn/location/',?fips)) AS ?loc), and bind ?loc as the SAME variable in the spoke-okn GRAPH block (?loc a ?t). NOTE: identify spoke-okn county nodes by '?loc a ?t' + the IRI pattern, NOT by a bound 'a <https://schema.org/AdministrativeArea>' constraint, which silently returns 0 on spoke-okn (base-file methodology_note / L2). The rebuilt-IRI shared-variable join sidesteps the FRINK cross-graph FILTER gotcha and avoids a full-graph string scan.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: fiokg shares 3,032 county FIPS with spoke-okn - marginally more than with spatialkg (3,031) because spoke-okn has national county coverage (incl. AK/HI/territories) while spatialkg is limited to the 48 contiguous states + DC. spoke-okn thus bridges fiokg's facility county data to its environmental-health county context and onward to the spatialkg hub via the shared county-FIPS key both carry (base-file K1). Same county-FIPS-to-spoke-okn pattern as geoconnex (L2), scales (L5) and nikg (L7).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a county, combine EPA FRS facility counts/types (fiokg) with spoke-okn's county-level environmental and health indicators, and roll up to state via the shared FIPS.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Both graphs classify facilities/manufacturers with NAICS but mint the NAICS IRI differently. fiokg: the object of ofPrimaryIndustry/ofSecondaryIndustry is http://w3id.org/fio/v1/naics#NAICS-{6-digit} (extract the bare code REPLACE(STR(?ind),'^.*naics#NAICS-([0-9]+)$','$1')). sudokn: http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/NAICS%20{code}-individual (extract REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*NAICS%20([0-9]+)-individual$','$1') - same as base-file I1). A direct IRI join returns 0; intersect the two bare numeric codes via two DISTINCT sub-SELECTs (one per named graph).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: fiokg attaches to the manufacturing cluster (I) on the bare NAICS code: 64 of sudokn's 66 distinct NAICS codes also appear among fiokg's 3,842 (near-complete containment of sudokn's manufacturing codes in fiokg's national, all-sector facility set). This puts fiokg in the same NAICS world as sudokn and securechainkg (base-file I1/I2). The join is at the industry-classification level, NOT on individual facility/company instances (fiokg facilities and sudokn manufacturers have unrelated IRIs).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a manufacturing NAICS industry, list SUDOKN small/medium manufacturers (capabilities, certificates) alongside the EPA FRS facilities (fiokg) in that same industry - and, via fiokg's county/S2 location, where those facilities sit on the spatial hub.

verified_crosswalks.note: 3-way intersection equals the GXA-spoke 2-way because pankgraph carries the full gene catalog.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: One-query gene dossier: expression (GXA) + associations (spoke-okn) + islet/pancreatic context (pankgraph).
Baseline tissue expression (GXA) for any spoke-okn gene plus its network/disease/compound associations.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: geoconnex carries county FIPS as the object of the GNIS county predicate, an https://geoconnex.us/ref/counties/{FIPS5} reference IRI. Extract the bare 5-digit FIPS: REPLACE(STR(?county),'^.*/counties/([0-9]{5}).*$','$1'). spatialkg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS5} node IRIs (constrain with 'a kwg:AdministrativeRegion_2', which DOES work on spatialkg, then REGEX the IRI for the trailing 5 digits). Intersect the two bare-FIPS strings via two DISTINCT sub-SELECTs (see methodology_note). NOTE this is a DIFFERENT key than the two recorded geoconnex non-joins (S2 Level-13 cells; SAWGraph PWS reference IRIs), both of which remain genuine non-joins.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS geoconnex's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub. 3,114 of geoconnex's 3,243 distinct county FIPS match a spatialkg AdministrativeRegion_2 county (spatialkg covers the 48 contiguous states + DC; the unmatched ~129 are AK/HI/PR/territory counties spatialkg does not load). Via this key geoconnex's hydrologic features (wells, streams, monitoring sites - placed in a county by the GNIS county predicate) roll up to spatialkg's S2 grid and admin-region hierarchy, making geoconnex a county-keyed member of the SAWGraph spatial world alongside sockg, ruralkg and spoke-okn.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Roll geoconnex hydrologic monitoring features up to the county, then place those counties on the spatialkg S2 grid / admin-region hierarchy to pull neighboring counties or state-level rollups.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: geoconnex carries state FIPS as the object of the GNIS state predicate, an https://geoconnex.us/ref/states/{FIPS2} reference IRI. Extract the bare 2-digit FIPS: REPLACE(STR(?st),'^.*/states/([0-9]{2}).*$','$1'). spatialkg states are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS2} nodes (constrain 'a kwg:AdministrativeRegion_1', REGEX the trailing 2 digits). Intersect the two bare-FIPS strings.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 51 states (50 states + DC) are common to geoconnex and spatialkg - a complete coarse state-level rollup key for geoconnex's hydrologic features.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Roll geoconnex hydrologic features up to the state and join state-level summaries to the spatialkg admin-region hierarchy.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same geoconnex bare-FIPS extract as L1. spoke-okn county nodes carry the 5-digit county FIPS as the node-IRI suffix (.../location/{FIPS5}); identify them by the IRI pattern '/location/[0-9]{5}$' (DO NOT gate on 'a <https://schema.org/AdministrativeArea>' as a bound IRI - that constraint silently returns 0 on spoke-okn; use '?loc a ?t' plus the IRI REGEX instead). Intersect the two bare-FIPS strings.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: geoconnex shares 3,184 county FIPS with spoke-okn - more than with spatialkg (3,114) because spoke-okn has national county coverage (incl. AK/HI/territories) while spatialkg is limited to the 48 contiguous states + DC. spoke-okn thus bridges geoconnex's hydrologic county data to its environmental-health county data and onward to the spatialkg hub via the shared county-FIPS key both carry (base-file K1).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a county, combine geoconnex's water-monitoring features with spoke-okn's local environmental/health context, and roll up to state via the shared FIPS.
Place Illinois wells/streams (hydrologykg) on the national S2 grid and roll up to county via spatialkg admin regions.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: NCI-PID proteins reference UniProt via owl:sameAs -> http://identifiers.org/uniprot/{ACC}; ProKN uses http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/{ACC}. Extract accession and rebuild the purl.uniprot.org form, then join on IRI existence.
verified_crosswalks.thin_thread_note: Small join: NCI-PID exposes only ~85 distinct UniProt ids via owl:sameAs (it is a focused signaling-pathway graph), of which 12 are present in ProKN. Real but low-cardinality; useful for pathway-context lookups, not bulk joins. NCI-PID also encodes some proteins as obo/PR_ terms that this CURIE route does not reach.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Signaling-pathway interaction proteins (NCI-PID) enriched with ProKN protein annotations.
NIAID datasets for a disease (NDE) plus its EHR phenotype signature (OARD).
verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. Joining on one position only undercounts (was 713, now 889).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: CORRECTS the base-file verdict that nikg 'exposes no geographic key at all'. nikg incident nodes carry a KWG sfWithin edge whose object is a phila.gov-REWRITTEN KWG admin-region IRI, https://metadata.phila.gov/kwgr_administrativeRegion_USA_{FIPS5} (it also carries census-tract objects of the form .../CT_{tractid}). Extract the bare county FIPS: REPLACE(STR(?o),'^.*administrativeRegion_USA_([0-9]{5})$','$1'). spatialkg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS5}. Intersect the bare-FIPS strings.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: nikg covers exactly two counties - Philadelphia (42101) and Cook/Chicago (17031) - and BOTH attach to the spatialkg hub. Small but real: it places nikg's Philadelphia/Chicago neighborhood-incident data (crime, arrests, officer-involved events) on the national S2 / admin-region hierarchy. nikg ALSO carries finer census-tract (CT_{id}) sfWithin objects not used here.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Place nikg Philadelphia/Chicago neighborhood-incident data on the spatialkg admin-region hierarchy and compare the two counties' state-level context.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same nikg phila.gov KWG-IRI FIPS extract as L6; match spoke-okn county nodes by their .../location/{FIPS5} IRI pattern. Intersect bare-FIPS strings.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: Both nikg counties (Philadelphia 42101, Cook 17031) are present in spoke-okn, joining nikg neighborhood-incident data to spoke-okn's county environmental/health context. (nikg is Philadelphia-centric; dreamkg's Philadelphia ZIP data - base-file J3 - is a related but ZIP-keyed neighbor, not directly joinable to nikg, whose geography is county + census tract, not ZIP.)
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Join nikg Philadelphia neighborhood-incident counts with spoke-okn's Philadelphia-County environmental/health indicators.
Phenotypes clinically associated with rare diseases (OARD) that also have protein/marker-gene evidence (ProKN).
verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. Joining on one position only undercounts (was 4841, now 4876).

verified_crosswalks.example_question: Diseases with both EHR phenotype associations (OARD) and marker-gene/protein evidence (ProKN).
verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. The prokn side is scoped to curated up:Disease (http://purl.uniprot.org/core/Disease) nodes: prokn also carries this seeAlso on its biolink EFO_0000651 'DiseaseOrPhenotype' association nodes (a looser phenotype construct), which would inflate the count; restricting to up:Disease keeps the join disease-entity-to-disease-entity. up:Disease-scoped count = 445 (any-subject, including phenotype-association nodes, is 954).
verified_crosswalks.complementary_note: One of FOUR complementary linkages for oard-kg<->prokn diseases on prokn up:Disease: direct MONDO (A3), and three ubergraph bridges OMIM (A12), Orphanet (A14), DOID (A15). Each reaches prokn diseases keyed in a different scheme; a disease annotated only in one scheme is reached only by that linkage. UNION all four for complete coverage (MONDO∪OMIM∪Orphanet∪DOID = 1094 distinct up:Disease).

verified_crosswalks.example_question: Diabetes/pancreas disease-associated genes (pankgraph) with their spoke-okn associations.

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: rdf:subject / rdf:object / rdfs:seeAlso / HAS_MARKER_GENE / biolink:target_for (gene as HGNC IRI)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: prokn keys genes ONLY on HGNC (no Entrez/Ensembl of its own), so the join goes through Wikidata: strip prokn's HGNC IRI to the bare numeric ?hnum; match a Wikidata gene item by wdt:P354 (HGNC ID) = ?hnum (bare-string literal); read that item's wdt:P351 (NCBI/Entrez gene ID) literal ?entrez; rebuild http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{entrez} and join spoke-okn. identifier-mappings does NOT carry HGNC; wikidata is the bridge. ~99% of prokn HGNC ids resolve to a Wikidata P354 item (e.g. 188/189 on biolink:target_for).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-okn Entrez genes reached from prokn's HGNC gene set via the Wikidata P354->P351 bridge (16,298 of spoke-okn's 16,346 genes). The same bridge reaches spoke-genelab (27,226) and, by the shared Entrez key, rdkg and biobricks-aopwiki (C5-C8). prokn carries ~38,328 distinct HGNC ids; most ride on rdf:subject/rdf:object (reified INDRA-evidence statements) and rdfs:seeAlso, not the direct biolink relations, so those predicates must be included to capture the full set. prokn also appears in the protein cluster (G1/G2) via UniProt - it carries marker genes as HGNC and proteins as UniProt.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Protein marker genes for a cell type / tissue (ProKN HAS_MARKER_GENE, HGNC) mapped via Wikidata to their spoke-okn network/disease/compound associations.
Rare-disease EHR phenotype profile (OARD) combined with contraindicated/treating drugs (RDKG, DrugBank).
verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. Joining on one position only undercounts (was 1444, now 2014).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: As C5: strip rdkg's 'ncbigene/' prefix to the bare id, rebuild spoke-genelab's http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id} node IRI, join on the rebuilt term.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (of rdkg's 9,326 genes) - near-complete containment of rdkg's gene set in spoke-genelab.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Rare-disease genes (rdkg) with their spaceflight/omics observations in spoke-genelab (NASA GeneLab).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same Entrez gene, different IRI form. Strip rdkg's node IRI to the bare id (up to and including 'ncbigene/') and rebuild spoke-okn's form: BIND(IRI(CONCAT('http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/', ?id))); join on the rebuilt term. Both sides type the gene as biolink:Gene.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (of rdkg's 9,326 biolink:Gene nodes); spoke-okn has 16,346 genes so the join is bounded by rdkg.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Rare-disease gene associations (rdkg: gene_associated_with_condition / has_gene) enriched with that gene's network/disease/compound associations in spoke-okn.
Aggregate Maine PFAS samples (sawgraph) to S2 cells and counties (spatialkg).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Shared key NCBITaxon (obo/NCBITaxon_). sawgraph carries its taxa as the subjects of rdfs:subClassOf edges; all 538 distinct NCBITaxon terms exist in ubergraph's taxonomy (100% overlap), so the join is total. For CATEGORY questions ('all birds', 'any amphibian') expand a clade inline with ubergraph's precomputed closure: ?taxon rdfs:subClassOf* <parent NCBITaxon term>, then join the expanded set to sawgraph — that expansion is the example USE, not the join's cardinality.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Expand a clade in ubergraph, pull SAWGraph contaminant-sampled species within it.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT sawgraph NCBITaxon terms present in ubergraph (538/538 = 100%). (Corrects a prior 77, which was a single arbitrary clade's worth of taxa, not the shared-key join.)

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Shared key NCBITaxon (obo/NCBITaxon_). spoke-genelab stores each gene's organism as a STRING literal holding the NCBITaxon IRI on the Gene.taxonomy node property (schema/taxonomy), NOT as a node — coerce it to an IRI with BIND(IRI(STR(?ts)) AS ?taxon) before joining ubergraph's taxonomy. All 9 distinct model-organism taxa (human 9606, mouse 10090, Arabidopsis 3702, C. elegans 6239, zebrafish 7955, Drosophila 7227, rat 10116, yeast 4932, Monodelphis domestica 13613) exist in ubergraph (9/9). For CATEGORY questions expand a clade inline via ?taxon rdfs:subClassOf* <parent NCBITaxon term> on ubergraph, then join to spoke-genelab. NOTE: spoke-genelab's microbial Organism class (spoke-genelab/node/N IRIs, label-only e.g. 'Cupriavidus') carries no NCBITaxon id and cannot join by id.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-genelab Gene.taxonomy NCBITaxon terms present in ubergraph (9/9 = 100%). These are the model-organism taxa of the differential-expression genes; the join lets ubergraph's taxonomy lineage/clade closure be applied to spoke-genelab genes.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Expand a clade in ubergraph (e.g. all rodents) and pull spoke-genelab spaceflight differential-expression genes whose organism falls within it.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare county FIPS as a NUMERIC literal, leading zero dropped (scales) ; http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS5} (spatialkg)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: CORRECTS the base-file verdict that scales is a geographic island: the curated 'fips:CountyCode' schema field IS unpopulated, but the SCALES IDB (Federal Judicial Center Integrated Data Base) field hasIdbCounty carries the genuine 5-digit county FIPS - stored as a NUMERIC literal (xsd:decimal/integer), so the leading zero is dropped (e.g. 6037.0 = California 06037 = Los Angeles County; 4013.0 = Arizona 04013 = Maricopa). Normalize: cast to integer then string (STR(xsd:integer(?county))), DROP the sentinel value '88888' (FJC 'unknown/out-of-US' code, the single most frequent value), then zero-pad any 4-digit result to 5 (IF(STRLEN(?ci)=4, CONCAT('0',?ci), ?ci)). Intersect this padded FIPS string against spatialkg's AdministrativeRegion_2 IRIs (REGEX trailing 5 digits).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS scales's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub, and a correction to its base-file island status. scales holds 3,129 distinct valid county FIPS on hasIdbCounty (near-complete national coverage of federal court cases by county of filing); 3,029 of them match a spatialkg county (the ~100 unmatched are AK/HI/PR/territory + the 88888 sentinel). Via this key federal criminal/civil court activity (charges, parties, dispositions) rolls up to the county and joins the SAWGraph spatial world - and meets ruralkg's rural health+justice indicators (base-file K2) on the same county key.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate SCALES federal court cases to the county of filing and place those counties on the spatialkg S2 grid / admin-region hierarchy for state-level or neighboring-county justice summaries.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare county FIPS as a NUMERIC literal, leading zero dropped (scales) ; https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/spoke-okn/location/{FIPS5} (spoke-okn)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same numeric->zero-padded-5-digit-FIPS normalization of scales hasIdbCounty as L4 (drop the 88888 sentinel). spoke-okn county nodes carry the FIPS as their .../location/{FIPS5} IRI suffix; match by IRI pattern, not by bound rdf:type. Intersect the padded FIPS strings.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: scales shares 3,096 county FIPS with spoke-okn (more than with spatialkg's contiguous-only 3,029, since spoke-okn has national county coverage). Lets federal court activity (scales) be joined to spoke-okn's county-level environmental/health context on the shared FIPS key.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a county, combine SCALES federal court case volume/charges with spoke-okn's county-level environmental and health indicators.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Both graphs classify manufacturers with the SAME predicate (SUDOKN:hasPrimaryNAICSClassifier) but mint the NAICS classifier IRI differently. securechainkg: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/naics-311111.0-inst' (lowercase 'naics-', a trailing '.0', suffix '-inst'). sudokn: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/NAICS%20332710-individual' (uppercase 'NAICS', %20 space, suffix '-individual'). A direct IRI join returns 0; extract the bare numeric code from each and join on it: securechainkg REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*/naics-([0-9]+)\\.0-inst$','$1'); sudokn REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*/NAICS%20([0-9]+)-individual$','$1').
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: The two KGs do NOT share manufacturer/company instance IRIs (securechainkg mints '...{name}-company-inst' from DHS/D&B/MSKG sources; sudokn mints '...{domain}-company-instance'); a direct company join is 0. They join on the industry-classification keys, not on individual firms. 35 of sudokn's 66 NAICS codes are also used in securechainkg (which spans 301 codes total).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a manufacturing NAICS industry, list SUDOKN small/medium manufacturers (capabilities, certificates, capacity) and SecureChain supply-chain participants (software/hardware products and their vulnerabilities) in that same industry.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: suppliesToIndustry targets a small shared SUDOKN industry-sector controlled vocabulary, again minted differently per graph. securechainkg: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/renewableenergy-inst' (lowercase, no spaces, '-inst'). sudokn: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/Renewable%20Energy-industry-individual' (Title Case, %20 spaces, '-industry-individual'). Direct IRI join is 0. Normalize each to a lowercase, space-free label and join on it: securechainkg LCASE(REPLACE(REPLACE(STR(?ind),'^.*/',''),'-inst$','')); sudokn LCASE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(STR(?ind),'^.*/',''),'-industry-individual$',''),'%20','')).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Cross-reference the industry sectors served by SUDOKN manufacturers with the industry sectors of SecureChain supply-chain participants (e.g. which suppliers to the aerospace or automotive sector appear in both graphs).

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: sockg is a THIRD spatial leaf on the SAWGraph S2 hub, alongside sawgraph (Maine PFAS, D2) and hydrologykg (Illinois wells/streams, D1). It materializes its S2 Level-13 cells as its own typed nodes in the identical KWG resource IRI form (http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id}), so the join to spatialkg's S2Cell_Level13 nodes is direct, no rewrite. All 1069 sockg S2 cells are present in spatialkg. Soil-carbon experiment sites are multi-state, so this leaf does not co-locate with the Maine/Illinois leaves; like them it joins via the hub, not leaf-to-leaf.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Place SOCKG soil-organic-carbon experiment sites on the national S2 grid (spatialkg) and roll them up to county/state admin regions.

verified_crosswalks.secondary_admin_levels: sockg also carries 36 AdministrativeRegion_1 (state) and 2 AdministrativeRegion_0 (country) nodes in the same KWG IRI form; the county level (62) is the most specific shared rollup key.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate SOCKG soil-carbon / GHG-flux measurements to the county (FIPS) level and join them to spatialkg's admin-region hierarchy for state-level summaries.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: None needed: both KGs mint gene nodes as the IDENTICAL non-standard Entrez (NCBI Gene) IRI http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id}. Join directly on the shared node IRI term (bind the same variable in both GRAPH blocks; do not FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez gene nodes common to both graphs (of spoke-okn's 16,346 biolink:Gene nodes, 16,326 are present in spoke-genelab). spoke-genelab carries the same Entrez IRIs.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: GeneLab spaceflight/omics gene observations (spoke-genelab) joined to that gene's network/disease/compound associations in spoke-okn, directly on the shared Entrez gene id.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: ubergraph CHEBI term -> hasDbXref CURIE 'cas:NNNN-NN-N' (lowercase). Rebuild biobricks IRI: IRI(CONCAT('http://identifiers.org/cas/', SUBSTR(STR(?casCurie),5))). CHEBI is canonical obo/CHEBI_ on both spoke-okn and ubergraph, no rewrite there. Verified count 496 distinct CAS reached spoke-okn CHEBI -> ubergraph hasDbXref 'cas:' -> biobricks-toxcast (reproducible; corrects a prior 498).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Tie a ToxCast chemical (CAS) to spoke-okn gene/pathway/disease associations for the same compound via CHEBI.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn diseases are biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI IS the DOID term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{id}); biomarkerkg carries the same obo/DOID_ IRI. Join directly on the shared node IRI (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT DOID diseases shared (of spoke-okn's 180). Pairs with the existing biomarkerkg<->prokn DOID join (A5).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Biomarkers for a disease (BiomarkerKB) alongside that disease's spoke-okn gene/compound associations, joined on DOID.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same shared-variable ZIP-literal join (cross-graph STR()=FILTER returns 0). dreamkg uses http://schema.org/postalCode (http) vs ruralkg's https; the literal VALUES join regardless of predicate scheme.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ALL 53 of dreamkg's (Philadelphia) ZIPs are present in spoke-okn. Small because dreamkg is a single-city homelessness-services graph; spoke-okn's national ZIP set fully contains it.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For Philadelphia ZIPs, list DREAM-KG homelessness/social services in a ZIP alongside spoke-okn's local context.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn diseases are biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI IS the DOID term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{id}); nde carries the same obo/DOID_ IRI. verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn keys diseases on DOID (obo/DOID_), oard-kg on MONDO (obo/MONDO_). Bridge through ubergraph: ?mondo skos:exactMatch ?doid (the MONDO term exact-matches the spoke-okn DOID IRI; note the direction — MONDO is subject, DOID object), then match ?mondo in oard-kg. The oboInOwl:hasDbXref CURIE route (MONDO hasDbXref 'DOID:{id}') gives identical coverage. spoke-okn has 180 DOID diseases, of which 28 reach a MONDO present in oard-kg.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-okn DOID diseases (of 180) that map to a MONDO present in oard-kg; the exactMatch and hasDbXref bridge routes both give 28. Attaches spoke-okn to the disease-cluster hub. (Corrects a prior 40, which no bridge route reproduces.)

verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. Joining on one position only undercounts (was 28, now 40).

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MONDO_ (oard-kg) ; https://www.omim.org/entry/{id} (prokn) — bridged on OMIM:{id} via ubergraph oboInOwl:hasDbXref
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: oard-kg keys diseases on MONDO (obo/MONDO_, biolink:object); prokn carries OMIM ids on rdfs:seeAlso as https://www.omim.org/entry/{id} (https + www). Bridge through ubergraph: the MONDO term carries an oboInOwl:hasDbXref CURIE literal 'OMIM:{id}' (10,038 distinct MONDO->OMIM xrefs). Strip the 'OMIM:' prefix and rebuild prokn's https://www.omim.org/entry/{id} IRI, then match it on prokn rdfs:seeAlso. prokn also carries non-numeric OMIM phenotype-series entries (.../entry/MTHU…) which have no MONDO xref and harmlessly do not match.

The prokn side is scoped to curated up:Disease (http://purl.uniprot.org/core/Disease) nodes: prokn also carries this seeAlso on its biolink EFO_0000651 'DiseaseOrPhenotype' association nodes (a looser phenotype construct), which would inflate the count; restricting to up:Disease keeps the join disease-entity-to-disease-entity. up:Disease-scoped count = 444 (any-subject, including phenotype-association nodes, is 500).
verified_crosswalks.complementary_note: One of FOUR complementary linkages for oard-kg<->prokn diseases on prokn up:Disease: direct MONDO (A3), and three ubergraph bridges OMIM (A12), Orphanet (A14), DOID (A15). Each reaches prokn diseases keyed in a different scheme; a disease annotated only in one scheme is reached only by that linkage. UNION all four for complete coverage (MONDO∪OMIM∪Orphanet∪DOID = 1094 distinct up:Disease).

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same shared-variable ZIP-literal join as J1. ruralkg ENDPOINT QUIRK: binding the predicate directly as <https://schema.org/postalCode> returns 0 even though the triples exist; match it with a variable plus FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?p),'postalCode')). ruralkg ZIPs are clean 5-digit xsd:string.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ALL 4,938 of ruralkg's ZIPs are present in spoke-okn (full containment). Separately ruralkg carries censusCounty as KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}, so it also meets spoke-okn at the county level (see K2) and bridges to the spatialkg hub.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same FIPS-extract-and-rebuild as K1. ruralkg's censusCounty OBJECT is already a KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI, so the rebuilt spoke-okn IRI joins it directly (shared-variable binding). ruralkg is national, so it matches more spoke-okn counties than spatialkg does.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: All 3,196 spoke-okn county nodes are present in ruralkg's censusCounty set; ruralkg's national county coverage fully contains spoke-okn's. ruralkg thus bridges spoke-okn county data to its rural health/justice indicators and (via the shared KWG county form) onward to the spatialkg hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Join spoke-okn county-level environmental/health rollups with ruralkg rural-resilience (health + justice) indicators for the same county FIPS.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same FIPS-extract-and-rebuild as K1. sockg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} nodes (base-file H2), so the rebuilt spoke-okn IRI joins directly.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 31 of sockg's ~62 soil-carbon experiment counties coincide with spoke-okn counties. Small but real; lets soil-organic-carbon / GHG-flux county context (sockg) meet spoke-okn's environmental-health county data on the same KWG county key both share with the hub.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same 2-digit-FIPS extract-and-rebuild as K4; join to sockg's AdministrativeRegion_1 state nodes (KWG form, base-file H2).

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: node-iri (.../location/{FIPS5}; a schema:AdministrativeArea county node, label '... County/Parish/Borough')
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn county nodes carry the 5-digit county FIPS as the node-IRI suffix (https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/spoke-okn/location/01001), NOT as a KWG IRI. spatialkg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} nodes. Extract the bare FIPS from the spoke-okn IRI (REPLACE(STR(?c),'^.*/location/([0-9]{5})$','$1')) and rebuild the KWG IRI (IRI(CONCAT('http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/administrativeRegion.USA.',?fips))), then use that rebuilt IRI as the SAME variable in the spatialkg GRAPH block. FRINK GOTCHA: a cross-graph 'FILTER(?a=?b)'/'FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)' over two GRAPH blocks silently returns 0; bind the same term in both blocks instead. Match the rebuilt KWG IRI by EXISTENCE in spatialkg (?reg a ?t, any type) — 27 of the 3,122 joined counties are typed as something other than AdministrativeRegion_2, so a strict AR_2 filter under-counts (3,095).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS spoke-okn's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub. (It supersedes the earlier 'spoke-okn does not attach to spatialkg' non-join, which was assessed only at the ZIP/ZipCodeArea level: spoke-okn has no KWG zip node and no S2 cell, but it DOES carry county FIPS in its node IRIs.) 3,122 of spoke-okn's 3,196 county nodes match a spatialkg county (spatialkg covers the 48 contiguous states + DC; the ~74 unmatched are AK/HI/PR/territory counties spatialkg does not load). Via this key spoke-okn rolls up to spatialkg's S2 grid and AdministrativeRegion hierarchy.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate spoke-okn ZIP-level environmental/health data to the county, then place those counties on the spatialkg S2 grid / admin-region hierarchy and pull neighboring counties or state rollups.

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: node-iri (.../location/{FIPS2}; a schema:AdministrativeArea state node, label = state name)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn state nodes carry the 2-digit state FIPS as the node-IRI suffix (.../location/22 = Louisiana). Extract it (REPLACE(STR(?s),'^.*/location/([0-9]{2})$','$1')) and rebuild the KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI; join to spatialkg's AdministrativeRegion_1 state nodes on that rebuilt IRI (shared variable).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 51 of spoke-okn's 52 state nodes (50 states + DC; spoke-okn additionally carries PR/territory) match spatialkg states. Coarse but complete state-level rollup key. Note spoke-okn ALSO carries a 2-letter postal state code (schema:state, e.g. 'FL') on its ZIP nodes; the 2-digit FIPS node IRI is the cleaner key for joining the KWG stack.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Roll spoke-okn data up to the state and join state-level summaries to the spatialkg admin-region hierarchy.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Join on the ZIP xsd:string literal. spoke-okn: the ZIP is the rdfs:label of EVERY .../location/{STATE}-{ZIP} node (~42,724 of them); identify zip nodes by the IRI pattern location/[A-Z]{2}-[0-9]+ (do NOT gate on rdfs:seeAlso 'ZipCodeArea' - only 978 nodes carry that, and it is an unusable HTML-anchor literal anyway). sudokn: SUDOKN:hasZipcodeValue mixes U.S. ZIPs with Canadian postal codes (e.g. 'M3C 1T5'); constrain to U.S. with FILTER(REGEX(STR(?z),'^[0-9]{5}$')) if needed. FRINK GOTCHA: a cross-graph 'FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)' over two GRAPH blocks silently returns 0; bind the SAME variable term in both GRAPH blocks (both are xsd:string).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: Joins on ZIP literals, not company/location IRIs. 12,329 of sudokn's ~12,914 U.S. ZIPs coincide with a spoke-okn ZIP (spoke-okn is a near-complete national ZIP reference, so the join is bounded by sudokn's coverage).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a ZIP, list the SUDOKN small/medium manufacturers there (NAICS, capabilities, certificates) alongside spoke-okn's local environmental/health context.
