verified_crosswalks.methodology_note: oard-kg disease/phenotype IRIs appear as the object of BOTH biolink:subject and biolink:object (a reified association can carry the entity on either side); the skeleton UNIONs both positions so the join is entity-complete. Previously subject-only (was 209, now 212).

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: https://schema.org/value (literal object of a blank node typed both schema:PropertyValue and kwg:S2Cell, carrying schema:name 's2Level13')
verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id} (spatialkg node IRI) ; bare decimal 64-bit S2 cell id as a string literal (ufokn schema:value)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: ufokn does NOT materialize its S2 cells as KWG resource IRIs - they are BLANK NODES typed both schema:PropertyValue and <https://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/ontology#S2Cell> (note: https + /lod/ontology#, the ufokn ontology IRI, which is NOT spatialkg's <http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/ontology/S2Cell_Level13> node-type IRI). Each blank node carries schema:name ('s2Level13' or 's2Level18'), schema:description ('S2 cell at level 13'/'...18') and schema:value = the cell's 64-bit S2 id as a DECIMAL STRING (e.g. '9813806808853118976'). spatialkg materializes the SAME decimal id as the suffix of its S2Cell_Level13 node IRI http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id} (base-file D1/D2/H1/N1), so the join is DIRECT with NO value rewrite: extract ufokn's schema:value, BIND(IRI(CONCAT('http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.', STR(?val))) AS ?cell), and reuse ?cell as the subject in the spatialkg GRAPH block ('?cell a kwg:S2Cell_Level13'). The BIND-injected IRI DOES push down into the spatialkg block (verified). TWO FRINK GOTCHAS make the ufokn side unqueryable the naive way and are why the base file scored this 0/unprofiled: (1) BLANK-NODE / CONSTANT-PREDICATE: a bound constant predicate on these nodes silently returns 0 - '?cell <https://schema.org/value> ?v' yields nothing even though '?cell ?p ?v' returns ?p = https://schema.org/value with the value; you MUST read the value via a variable predicate (then FILTER on STR(?p) or, cheaper, FILTER the object by shape: isLiteral && REGEX(STR(?val),'^[0-9]{18,20}$')). (2) LARGE-GRAPH: ufokn has ~11.7M S2Cell-typed nodes; any unbounded DISTINCT/FILTER scan times out at 120s. Scope the ufokn side in an inner sub-SELECT '{ SELECT ?cell WHERE { GRAPH ufokn { ?cell a kwg:S2Cell } } LIMIT N }', THEN pull '?cell ?p ?val' with the regex object-filter, THEN rebuild and join to the light spatialkg side. Level-18 values harmlessly do not match (they are not level-13 ids), so no name-filter is required for correctness - feed all schema:value objects and the intersection is exactly the level-13 overlap.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: EXACT full level-13 join, re-derived via skeleton_query over all ufokn s2Level13 PropertyValue nodes (no sampling). Supersedes the prior 7,670 figure, which was an explicit LOWER BOUND from a 20,000-node sample; that sample showed 100% spatialkg containment, now confirmed across all 97,087 distinct level-13 cells. The full COUNT needs ~4 min, beyond a 90s tool call, so scope/paginate when querying interactively.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ufokn's primary - and so far only confirmed - attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub, making it a FIFTH S2 Level-13 leaf on spatialkg alongside sawgraph (Maine PFAS, 88,007; D2), hydrologykg (Illinois wells/streams, 106,138; D1), sockg (multi-state soil carbon, 1,069; H1) and fiokg (national EPA FRS facilities, 681,045; N1). Because ufokn's coverage is the continental US, it co-occupies cells with the national leaf fiokg broadly and with the Maine/Illinois leaves within those states; like the other leaves it meets them through the hub (D1/H1 pattern), and a direct leaf-to-leaf join was NOT separately verified here (regional sampling makes a leaf-to-leaf count unreliable - assert it only via the hub). Via this key every ufokn Risk-Point / built-environment feature rolls up to spatialkg's S2 grid and AdministrativeRegion hierarchy and meets the other S2 leaves in any shared cell. ufokn ALSO carries finer S2 LEVEL-18 cells (schema:name 's2Level18'); spatialkg's grid is level-13, so level-18 cells do not attach to the hub directly (they nest inside the matched level-13 parent).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Place urban-flooding Risk-Points and built-environment features (ufokn) on the national S2 Level-13 grid (spatialkg), roll them up to county/state admin regions, and find which flood risk cells also contain EPA FRS facilities (fiokg), PFAS samples (sawgraph) or hydrologic features (hydrologykg).

cluster_notes.A_disease: spoke-okn keys diseases on DOID (180 biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI is the obo/DOID_ term). It joins the DOID carriers DIRECTLY on the shared DOID IRI: prokn 96 (A7), biomarkerkg 79 (A8), nde 20 (A9; nde's schema:healthCondition holds DOID alongside MONDO/HP/NCIT). The MONDO-keyed members need a DOID->MONDO bridge through ubergraph (?mondo skos:exactMatch ?doid, equivalently oboInOwl:hasDbXref 'DOID:{id}' - both give identical coverage): rdkg 149 (A10, the broadest spoke-okn disease join since rdkg is rare-disease-rich), oard-kg 40 (A11, attaching spoke-okn to the disease-cluster hub; the existing DOID pair biomarkerkg<->prokn is A5). Every federation disease KG is thus reachable from spoke-okn: DOID carriers directly, MONDO members via ubergraph. Caveat: spoke-okn's disease set is small (180), so counts are bounded by spoke-okn.
cluster_notes.C_gene_cluster: The gene cluster spans THREE id systems. (1) ENTREZ / NCBI Gene is the federation's de-facto gene join key, but in THREE IRI FORMS: http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id} (spoke-okn and spoke-genelab gene NODES; spoke-okn types them biolink:Gene, 16,346 nodes), http://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id} (rdkg gene nodes), and https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id} (biobricks-aopwiki, as skos:exactMatch on key-event target genes). Same numeric id - join by stripping to the bare integer and rebuilding the partner's form (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks; never FILTER(STR(?a)=?b), which returns 0 here). Verified Entrez pairs: spoke-okn<->spoke-genelab (C4, 16,326), rdkg<->spoke-okn (C5, 8,475), rdkg<->spoke-genelab (C6, 9,034), aopwiki<->spoke-genelab (C7, 1,472), aopwiki<->rdkg (C8, 1,193). C7 is notable: spoke-genelab is Entrez-only, so AOP-Wiki - previously tied to the cluster only on Ensembl (F1/F2) - reaches it only via this Entrez route. (2) ENSEMBL members are gene-expression-atlas-okn, pankgraph and biobricks-aopwiki, plus spoke-okn via its schema/ensembl crosswalk (16,316 of its 16,346 genes carry an http://identifiers.org/ensembl/{ENSG} value). pankgraph is Ensembl-ONLY (its gene nodes are ensembl IRIs with no Entrez xref; only expressed_in->CL and functional_association->GO edges). The C1-C3/F1 joins to spoke-okn remain valid and unchanged, but the spoke-okn side is reached via the schema/ensembl predicate (Ensembl as object), not the gene node. (3) HGNC is carried ONLY by prokn (http://identifiers.org/hgnc/{N}, ~38,328 distinct ids, mostly on rdf:subject/rdf:object reified INDRA statements and rdfs:seeAlso). prokn has no Entrez/Ensembl of its own, so it bridges to the Entrez world through the WIKIDATA graph: HGNC bare id -> wdt:P354 -> same item's wdt:P351 (Entrez literal) -> rebuild the spoke Entrez IRI (C9: prokn<->spoke-okn 16,298, prokn<->spoke-genelab 27,226; ~99% of prokn HGNC ids resolve via P354). Note identifier-mappings does NOT carry HGNC - wikidata is the bridge. prokn is also a protein-cluster member (G1/G2, UniProt): it holds marker genes as HGNC and proteins as UniProt. HINGES: spoke-okn ties the Entrez and Ensembl sub-worlds together (it alone carries both - Entrez node + Ensembl crosswalk); wikidata ties HGNC to Entrez. So every gene member is reachable from every other: the Entrez core (spoke-okn, spoke-genelab, rdkg, aopwiki) directly, the Ensembl members (GXA, pankgraph) via spoke-okn's crosswalk, and prokn (HGNC) via wikidata.
cluster_notes.E_toxicology_cas: biobricks-tox21 and biobricks-aopwiki both attach to the CAS chemical cluster anchored by biobricks-toxcast / biobricks-ice (base file B1). tox21 joins natively (same http://identifiers.org/cas/ node-IRI form); AOP-Wiki needs the https->http rewrite. This makes AOP-Wiki a hinge: its stressors carry CAS (chemical cluster) AND its key-event targets carry Ensembl + UniProt (gene/protein clusters).
cluster_notes.F_aopwiki_gene_bridge: AOP-Wiki's skos:exactMatch block carries genes as BOTH Entrez (~1,480 https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/ ids) and Ensembl (~1,478 https://identifiers.org/ensembl/ENSG ids), plus hmdb/chebi/pubchem.compound/drugbank chemical ids. F1/F2 use its Ensembl route (to spoke-okn and GXA); the Entrez route is now verified too and links the Entrez-only member spoke-genelab (C7) and rdkg (C8). Standard https->http (or bare-id rebuild) normalization applies.
cluster_notes.G_protein_cluster: UniProt accession is a federation-wide protein key but appears in at least three IRI forms: http://identifiers.org/uniprot/ (ncipidkg), https://identifiers.org/uniprot/ (aopwiki), and http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/ (prokn, which models proteins with the UniProt core ontology). Always join on the bare accession, never the raw IRI.
cluster_notes.H_soil_spatial: sockg (Soil Organic Carbon KG) joins the SAWGraph spatial hub (base D-series) as a third leaf next to sawgraph (Maine PFAS) and hydrologykg (Illinois). It reuses the SAWGraph/KWG spatial stack wholesale: S2Cell_Level13 cells, GADM AdministrativeRegion_0..2 nodes, kwg:sfWithin / sfContains, spatial-full:connectedTo, and GeoSPARQL geometries — all in native KWG IRI forms, so attachment to spatialkg needs no rewrite. Note: sockg ALSO carries contaminoso:casNumber (coso/contaminoso vocabulary) on its chemical entities, the same CAS chemical world as base-file B1 (toxcast/ice) and the E-series — a real thread into the chemical cluster that is intentionally NOT expanded here (out of scope: chemical/tox is already covered).
cluster_notes.I_manufacturing_supply_chain: securechainkg embeds the SUDOKN manufacturing ontology (http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/*) almost in full on its Manufacturer entities: hasPrimaryNAICSClassifier, suppliesToIndustry, hasProcessCapability, hasMaterialCapability, organizationLocatedIn, hasCertificate, hasNumberOfEmployees, hasOrganizationYearOfEstablishment. This makes SecureChain and SUDOKN siblings on the manufacturing side. They share the industry-classification vocabularies (NAICS codes I1; industry sectors I2) but NOT the individual company instances (different IRI minting), so joins are at the industry-class level. Beyond the manufacturing overlap, securechainkg adds a software-supply-chain layer with no cross-KG partner in the federation: Software / SoftwareVersion / Vulnerability (CVE-style ids via schema:identifier), Hardware / HardwareVersion (CPE 2.3 via secure-chain:cpe23), license (schema:license) and dependsOn / vulnerableTo edges. These are useful securechainkg-internal join keys but have no second graph to bridge to here.
cluster_notes.J_spoke_okn_zip_geography: spoke-okn carries ~42,724 ZIP nodes (.../location/{STATE}-{ZIP}), ALL with a bare-ZIP rdfs:label (xsd:string) - the clean cross-KG key. (Only 978 also have a KWG ZipCodeArea rdfs:seeAlso, stored as an HTML-anchor literal and unusable as an IRI; do not gate on it - that was the bug that undercounted the first version of this file.) Partners with bare 5-digit ZIPs join directly: sudokn (hasZipcodeValue, 12,329), ruralkg (schema:postalCode, 4,938 = all of ruralkg), dreamkg (schema:postalCode, 53 = all of dreamkg). For coarser geography spoke-okn also exposes county FIPS and state FIPS in its node IRIs - see the admin-region file (cluster K), including its attachment to the spatialkg hub. Pervasive failure mode: the FRINK cross-graph literal-join quirk (FILTER(STR(?a)=?b) yields 0; bind the same variable term in both GRAPH blocks).
cluster_notes.K_spoke_okn_admin_region_fips: spoke-okn's geographic hierarchy is fully FIPS-coded above the ZIP level, with the code living in the node-IRI suffix (.../location/{FIPS5} for counties, .../location/{FIPS2} for states) rather than in any KWG/identifiers.org IRI. Because the SAWGraph spatial stack (spatialkg hub; sockg and the sawgraph/hydrologykg leaves; ruralkg's censusCounty) keys admin regions on KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}, the join is: extract the bare FIPS from spoke-okn -> rebuild the KWG IRI -> match as a shared-variable term. County FIPS is the strongest spoke-okn geographic key in the federation (national, 3,196 counties): spatialkg 3,122 (= spoke-okn's attachment to the hub), ruralkg 3,196 (full containment), sockg 31. State FIPS adds a coarse rollup (spatialkg 51, sockg 18). IMPORTANT CORRECTIONS to the prior files: (1) the earlier 'spoke-okn <-> spatialkg = 0 / no hub attachment' non-join was a ZIP-level result only; via county/state FIPS spoke-okn DOES attach to the hub (K1, K4). (2) The ZIP cluster (J) counts in spoke-okn-geolocation-crosswalks.json were gated on the 978 ZIP nodes that carry a KWG ZipCodeArea reference, but spoke-okn actually has ~42,724 ZIP nodes ALL bearing a bare-ZIP rdfs:label; recomputed on the full set the ZIP joins are far larger - sudokn 12,329 (was 387), ruralkg 4,938 (was 178), dreamkg 53 (was 4) - i.e. spoke-okn is effectively a complete national ZIP/county/state reference and the join is bounded by the PARTNER's coverage, not spoke-okn's. CITY level does not join: spoke-okn's ~67,909 city/place nodes are name slugs with no FIPS/GEOID (see known_non_joins).
cluster_notes.L_county_fips_hub_attachments: Cluster L adds three county-FIPS-keyed members to the SAWGraph spatial world, each reached through a DIFFERENT non-obvious encoding of the same key: geoconnex (hydrology; FIPS as https://geoconnex.us/ref/counties/{FIPS5} reference IRIs on the GNIS county predicate; 3,114 counties to spatialkg / 3,184 to spoke-okn / 51 states), scales (federal court justice data; FIPS as a NUMERIC hasIdbCounty literal with the leading zero dropped, sentinel 88888 removed; 3,029 to spatialkg / 3,096 to spoke-okn), and nikg (Philadelphia/Chicago neighborhood incidents; FIPS in a phila.gov-rewritten KWG sfWithin IRI; 2 counties). All three join on the SAME county-FIPS key the K-cluster uses, so they also meet ruralkg (K2), sockg (K3) and each other at the county level via the hub. Three base-file verdicts are thereby corrected: scales (was a geographic island on the unpopulated fips:CountyCode field), nikg (was 'no geographic key at all'), and geoconnex (only non-joins were recorded; those non-joins on S2 cells and PWS reference IRIs remain valid, but the county-FIPS key is a separate, working route to the hub).
cluster_notes.M_mesh_vocabulary: Cluster M attaches biobricks-mesh - a standalone NLM MeSH vocabulary whose entities are their own id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/{ID} descriptor IRIs and which carries no outbound xref of its own - to the federation via TWO complementary routes. M1 (broad, indirect): ubergraph cross-references 10,004 MeSH ids as oboInOwl:hasDbXref 'MESH:{ID}' literals; 9,463 of biobricks-mesh's descriptors match (8,202 MONDO disease, 1,051 UBERON anatomy, 83 CL cell type), so through MONDO biobricks-mesh joins the disease cluster A (oard-kg/rdkg/prokn/nde and, via DOID<->MONDO, spoke-okn's disease ontology). M2 (narrow, direct): spoke-okn itself MeSH-tags ~165 nodes through mesh_ids / mesh_list - 155 Disease + 10 SDoH - all matching biobricks-mesh, with NO bridge needed. The two routes are not redundant: M2 uniquely reaches spoke-okn's SDoH (social-determinants) nodes, which have no MONDO term and so are invisible to M1. Watch the IRI gotchas: ubergraph uses 'MESH:{ID}' literals (M1) and spoke-okn uses HTTPS mesh IRIs vs biobricks-mesh's HTTP (M2) - both need bare-id normalization. Overlap check (M2 vs spoke-okn's DOID disease join): all 180 spoke-okn Disease nodes are DOID-identified and 159 also carry a MeSH tag, so for diseases M2 and the DOID join hit the same entities (M2 supplies a MeSH<->DOID map on shared nodes, not new diseases); only the 10 SDoH nodes are unique to M2. Cross-route validation (M2 vs M1): bridging spoke's DOID->MONDO via ubergraph, 118/164 of the spoke MeSH<->DOID disease pairs are exactly corroborated by ubergraph's independent MeSH->MONDO xref, 32 are cases ubergraph has no MeSH (M2 adds coverage), and only 14 disagree - all semantically adjacent (organism-vs-disease or parent/child granularity), no flat errors. MeSH is a high-confidence bridge for exact ids but approximate at the leaves, especially for infections; see M2.cross_route_validation.
cluster_notes.N_fiokg_spatial_and_naics: fiokg (SAWGraph FRS KG; EPA Facility Registry Service) attaches to the federation on TWO axes. (1) SPATIAL - it is a full SAWGraph spatial leaf: every facility links via owl:sameAs to native KWG/STKO resource IRIs (the same stack as spatialkg, sawgraph, hydrologykg, sockg), so attachment needs NO rewrite. It carries 681,108 S2 Level-13 cells (681,045 in spatialkg - the federation's largest S2 join, N1) and 3,040 clean county-FIPS KWG admin-region IRIs (3,031 in spatialkg, N2; 3,032 in spoke-okn, N3). It is the FOURTH and largest S2 leaf on the hub and, being national, the only facility-scale one. It attaches ONLY at the county + S2 levels (0 state KWG IRIs). Because fiokg and sawgraph are both KWG-S2 leaves and fiokg is national while sawgraph is Maine, they OVERLAP directly in 4,712 Maine cells (N6) - a true leaf-to-leaf join, not just a hub rendezvous - which is valuable since fiokg flags 188,057 EPA-PFAS-Facility instances against sawgraph's PFAS samples. (2) INDUSTRY - fiokg classifies facilities with NAICS (ofPrimaryIndustry / ofSecondaryIndustry -> http://w3id.org/fio/v1/naics#NAICS-{6-digit}); the bare 6-digit code joins the manufacturing/supply-chain cluster (I): 64 of sudokn's 66 codes (N4) and all 301 of securechainkg's codes (N5) are contained in fiokg's 3,842-code national span. The join is at the industry-CLASS level (shared NAICS code), not on facility/company instances, exactly as securechainkg<->sudokn (I1). DATA-QUALITY NOTE: of fiokg's 3,301 distinct admin-region owl:sameAs objects, 261 (256 three-digit + 5 four-digit) are leading-zero-truncated and do not match the zero-padded KWG hub form; filter to the 5-digit suffix for the county join (see known_non_joins).
cluster_notes.O_ufokn_s2: ufokn (Urban Flooding OKN) attaches to the SAWGraph spatial hub on S2 Level-13 cells, but via a non-obvious encoding that defeated the base-file scan. Its S2 cells are blank nodes (typed schema:PropertyValue + ufokn's own kwg:S2Cell ontology class), not KWG resource IRIs, and the cell id lives in schema:value as a bare decimal 64-bit integer - the IDENTICAL value spatialkg uses as the suffix of its S2Cell_Level13 node IRI. Rebuilding s2.level13.{value} and joining to spatialkg's typed nodes is direct (no value rewrite). The base file recorded ufokn as 'unprofiled-large' and 'S2 = 0'; both are superseded here (the 0 was a direct typed-NODE join against blank nodes, which can never match; the timeout was an unbounded scan of ~11.7M S2 nodes). Re-profiled with a type-bounded sub-SELECT + object-shape regex filter as the base file advised. All three samples show 100% containment of ufokn level-13 cells in spatialkg, so this is a near-complete leaf attachment whose exact cardinality just isn't enumerable within the endpoint's time limit.
cluster_notes.P_pubchem_cid: Cluster P attaches biobricks-pubchem-annotations - a 14.4M-compound PubChem annotation graph (oa:Annotation nodes whose oa:hasTarget / dc:subject objects are http://rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/compound/CID{n} IRIs, each annotation carrying free-text hazard/toxicity/use text on its body) - to the federation via PubChem CID. The original profiling pass recorded it as an island because no chemical-cluster KG (biobricks-ice/tox21/toxcast, keyed on CAS+DTXSID) and not identifier-mappings (CID-free) carries a CID. That pass missed spoke-okn: its 798 biolink:ChemicalEntity nodes expose the CID as oboInOwl:hasDbXref objects in http://identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{n} form (762 distinct), a sibling IRI form of the same CID. A naive IRI join returns 0 (form drift: rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/.../CID{n} vs identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{n}); strip both to the bare numeric CID to join. Verified exact 2026-06-11: 762/762 of spoke-okn's CID-bearing compounds (100%) have >=1 biobricks annotation - e.g. Atrazine CID 2256, Diuron CID 3120, Phenytoin CID 1775, Fluorouracil CID 3385. spoke-okn is the ONLY federation partner on PubChem CID. NB spoke-okn ALSO references PubChem as InChIKey IRIs (762 ChemicalEntity node IRIs + 55 INTERACTS_CiC/PARTOF_CpC edge objects, http://rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/inchikey/{KEY}); that is a DIFFERENT key and does NOT match biobricks' CID - use the hasDbXref CID route, not the node-IRI InChIKey route. This SUPERSEDES the base file's biobricks-pubchem-annotations island / PubChem-CID known_non_join.

rollup_keys: S2 Level-13 cell as KWG IRI: http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{decimal-64bit-id}
Census GEOID as Data Commons IRI: https://datacommons.org/browser/geoId/{geoid}
spatial_hub_note.topology: spatialkg is the national S2 grid + admin-region hub. Its S2 Level-13 leaves are sawgraph (Maine; D2), hydrologykg (Illinois; D1), sockg (multi-state; H1), fiokg (national; N1) and now ufokn (continental US; O1). Leaves that share a state co-occupy cells (e.g. fiokg<->sawgraph in Maine, N6); otherwise they meet through the hub. spoke-okn and the cluster K/L members attach at the county and state admin-region levels (national county coverage).
spatial_hub_note.spoke_okn_attachment: CONFIRMED: spoke-okn attaches to the spatialkg hub via county FIPS (3,122; K1) and state FIPS (51; K4), by rebuilding the KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI from spoke-okn's bare-FIPS node IRIs. It does NOT attach via ZIP (spatialkg has no ZipCodeArea nodes) or S2 cell (spoke-okn has none of its own). spoke-okn is now a THIRD admin-region-keyed member of the SAWGraph spatial world alongside sockg, sawgraph and hydrologykg - and the only one with national county coverage.
spatial_hub_note.cluster_L_county_members: Cluster L adds three more county-FIPS-keyed members to this hub, each via a different non-obvious encoding of the same key: geoconnex (hydrology; 3,114 counties to spatialkg / 3,184 to spoke-okn / 51 states; L1-L3), scales (federal-court justice data via numeric hasIdbCounty, sentinel 88888 dropped; 3,029 to spatialkg / 3,096 to spoke-okn; L4-L5), and nikg (Philadelphia 42101 + Cook/Chicago 17031 via phila.gov-rewritten KWG sfWithin IRIs; L6-L7). All join on the same county-FIPS key as cluster K, so they also meet ruralkg (K2), sockg (K3), spoke-okn (K1) and each other at the county level.
spatial_hub_note.fiokg_attachment: CONFIRMED: fiokg attaches to the spatialkg hub via S2 Level-13 cells (681,045; N1 - the largest S2 join in the federation) and county FIPS (3,031 AdministrativeRegion_2; N2), both in native KWG resource IRI form requiring NO rewrite. It does NOT attach via state (no KWG AdministrativeRegion_1 IRIs) or ZIP. fiokg is the FOURTH S2-keyed leaf on the hub alongside sawgraph (Maine), hydrologykg (Illinois) and sockg (multi-state soil carbon) - and, with national facility coverage, by far the largest. It also meets spoke-okn at the county level (3,032; N3, spoke-okn's national county coverage). Unusually, fiokg and the sawgraph leaf OVERLAP directly in 4,712 Maine S2 cells (N6) rather than only meeting through the hub.
spatial_hub_note.ufokn_attachment: CONFIRMED: ufokn attaches to the spatialkg hub via S2 Level-13 cells (O1), making it the FIFTH S2-keyed leaf alongside sawgraph, hydrologykg, sockg and fiokg. Unlike the others, ufokn's S2 cells are BLANK NODES (not KWG resource IRIs); the cell id is the bare decimal 64-bit integer in schema:value, which is rebuilt into spatialkg's s2.level13.{id} IRI for a direct join (no rewrite). All sampled level-13 cells are present in spatialkg (100% containment across 1k/8k/20k-cell samples), so the attachment is near-complete; the exact count is not enumerable within the 120s limit (verified_count 7,670 is a sampled lower bound). A county/state admin-region or geometry route for ufokn was NOT profiled and is left open. ufokn also carries finer S2 Level-18 cells that nest inside the level-13 hub cells but do not attach to the level-13 hub directly.

methodology_note: FRINK FEDERATION GOTCHAS used throughout cluster L (learned while verifying these joins): (1) a constant-IRI constraint in a remote GRAPH block can silently return 0 - e.g. '?loc a <https://schema.org/AdministrativeArea>' returns 0 on spoke-okn even though '?loc a ?type' returns the nodes with ?type = that exact IRI; match the county/state nodes by their IRI PATTERN (REGEX on the node IRI) instead of by a bound rdf:type IRI. (2) A VALUES- or BIND-injected IRI is NOT reliably pushed down into a remote GRAPH block, so 'BIND(IRI(...)) AS ?loc . GRAPH <other-kg> { ?loc a ?t }' also returns 0. (3) The reliable cross-graph join is on a BARE STRING KEY: extract the 5-digit county FIPS (or 2-digit state FIPS) as an xsd:string on EACH side independently (each inside its own DISTINCT sub-SELECT scoped to one named graph) and intersect the two sub-SELECTs on that shared ?fips variable. Never use a cross-graph FILTER(STR(?a)=?b) - it returns 0 (same gotcha the base file records for K1). CLUSTER N ADDENDUM: fiokg's owl:sameAs predicate carries ~681k S2-cell objects, so any scan touching the whole predicate (CONTAINS over all of it, or joining two heavy graphs through it) can hit the 120s endpoint limit; scope each side in its own DISTINCT sub-SELECT and keep the partner light - for spoke-okn (N3) rebuild the .../location/{FIPS5} node IRI from fiokg's extracted FIPS and bind it as the same variable rather than regex-scanning spoke-okn. For NAICS (N4/N5) a direct IRI join is 0 (each KG mints the classifier IRI differently); extract the bare numeric code on each side and intersect. CLUSTER O ADDENDUM (ufokn): ufokn's S2 cells are BLANK NODES typed schema:PropertyValue + its own <https://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/ontology#S2Cell> class (NOT spatialkg's S2Cell_Level13 node-type IRI), so the base file's direct typed-NODE join returns 0 by construction - there is no shared node IRI. The shared key is the schema:value LITERAL (bare decimal 64-bit S2 id), joined by rebuilding spatialkg's s2.level13.{value} IRI; that BIND/CONCAT IRI DOES push down into the spatialkg block here (verified), unlike gotcha (2) elsewhere. Two further traps: (a) a CONSTANT predicate on these blank nodes silently returns 0 - '?cell <https://schema.org/value> ?v' yields nothing though '?cell ?p ?v' returns ?p = that exact IRI with the value; read the value via a VARIABLE predicate and select it by object SHAPE (isLiteral && REGEX(STR(?val),'^[0-9]{18,20}$')) rather than by a bound predicate. (b) LARGE-GRAPH: ~11.7M S2-typed nodes; any unbounded DISTINCT/FILTER over the class times out at 120s (>=60k-cell bounded samples can too). Scope ufokn in an inner 'SELECT ?cell ... LIMIT N' sub-SELECT first, then pull+shape-filter the value, then join to the light spatialkg side. Level-18 values harmlessly fail the level-13 membership test, so no name/level filter is needed for a correct level-13 intersection.

known_non_joins.diagnosis: SAWGraph owl:sameAs points to geoconnex.us/ref/pws/ reference IRIs that are NOT materialized as subjects in the geoconnex snapshot (tested http/https/reference. forms). Reference-IRI vs materialized-node mismatch; not fixable by rewrite. Do not retry.

identifier-mappings does not carry identifiers.org/cas/ or identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/ IRIs (bound lookups returned zero). Use the ubergraph CHEBI route (B2) to bridge the chemical clusters, not identifier-mappings/wikidata.
Named graph https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/digcfdekg returns zero triples at this endpoint. Nothing to join.

find_crosswalks returns empty: no mapping predicates, no ontology/identifier node IRIs, no domain-predicate ids. EvoWeb (co-evolving genes) encodes its gene/cluster entities as custom IRIs, so it shares no off-the-shelf join key with the gene/protein clusters. Would need a dedicated gene xref table. UNVERIFIED.

0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes. The NASA GES DISC KG models datasets/instruments/platforms/science-keywords rather than gridded geography, so it does not attach to the S2 spatial hub. Any join would be via DOI (datasets) or GCMD science keywords, not the spatial hub — not pursued here (the natural DOI/keyword partners are publication/biomedical graphs).

0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes; no S2 hub attachment. (A taxon-based route to ubergraph/sawgraph in the spirit of base D3 might exist but was not explored — species/taxonomy is biology-adjacent and out of scope here.)

Climate Models KG is a self-contained custom ontology (climatepub4kg.github.io/ontology#*). Its geography is modeled as bespoke classes (City, Country, Country_Subdivision, Continent) with no KWG admin-region, S2, or FIPS IRIs, and 0 S2Cell_Level13 nodes shared with spatialkg. The only outward-facing handles are literal node properties — Variable.cf_standard_name (CF standard names) and Paper.id/title — neither an IRI join key. Effectively an island for IRI-level federation; a literal cf_standard_name match to NASA/GCMD keyword text is the only conceivable thin thread.

At the ZIP level spoke-okn does not reach spatialkg: spoke-okn references KWG ZipCodeArea only as HTML-anchor literals and spatialkg materializes no ZipCodeArea nodes (and spoke-okn has no S2 cell). NOTE: this is ZIP-level only - spoke-okn DOES attach to spatialkg via county FIPS (K1) and state FIPS (K4).

securechainkg embeds much of the SUDOKN ontology but does not populate SUDOKN:hasZipcodeValue in the reachable slice, so there is no ZIP-level location to join. Other location encodings were not confirmable (graph large, scans time out) - UNVERIFIED rather than proven absent.
PHASES KG carries no zip/postal/FIPS predicate in the slice scanned.

spoke-okn's ~67,909 city/place nodes (.../location/{name-slug}, labels like 'Outside city limits') carry NO FIPS place code, GEOID, or other external identifier - only a name. A city-level join would have to be on the city NAME string, which is unreliable across the federation (same name in many states, inconsistent casing/abbreviation, 'Outside city limits' placeholders), so there is no clean key-based city crosswalk. Use the county (K1-K3) or state (K4-K5) FIPS level for reliable joins; drop to ZIP (cluster J) for finer geography.

A 150,000-triple profile of BiomarkerKB surfaced only DOID (disease), CHEBI (chemical) and CL (cell type) ids on its OBCI domain predicates - no standard gene IRIs. If biomarker->gene links exist they are not on an off-the-shelf gene id here; re-profile its biomarker/gene predicates specifically before assuming a gene join. (evoweb and digcfdekg, the other gene-adjacent KGs, are already listed above as non-joins.)

known_non_joins.attempted_key: FDA device product code / device name / disease / chemical / geography
known_non_joins.diagnosis: FDA MAUDE adverse-event KG. Only two node types (MAUDEReport 15,284 ; MedicalDevice 4,099) and only medicaldevice.com/ontology predicates (productCode, eventType, involvesDevice, dateReceived, dateOfEvent, label). Its join candidates are FDA 3-letter device product codes (e.g. 'HRS','EMJ') as plain string literals and free-text device IRIs (medicaldevice.com/resource/device/{name}). No federation KG carries FDA device product codes, and find_crosswalks returns empty (no mapping predicates, no ontology/identifier node IRIs). eventType is only Malfunction/Injury/Death (no MONDO/HP). Island.

known_non_joins.attempted_key: DOI / GCMD science keyword / admin region
known_non_joins.diagnosis: NASA GES DISC Earth-science KG (publications, datasets, instruments, platforms, projects, science keywords). Its dcterms:identifier values are DOIs - NASA dataset DOIs (10.5067/...) and journal-article DOIs (10.1111/..., 10.1016/...) - but NO federation KG is DOI-keyed, so there is no DOI partner. HAS_SCIENCEKEYWORD objects are internal .../kg/node/{id} IRIs (not shared GCMD-keyword IRIs). Geography is raw lat/long bounding-box corners (nwCorner/seCorner), not admin regions, GeoNames ids, or S2 cells, so it does not reach the SAWGraph hub. The other Earth-science graph, climatemodelskg, is keyed on GeoNames ids + CF standard variable names, which nasa-gesdisc-kg does not carry - no overlap. Island within this federation.

known_non_joins.attempted_key: GeoNames id / CF standard variable name / science keyword
known_non_joins.diagnosis: The two Earth-science KGs do not share a materialized key. climatemodelskg geo-locates on GeoNames (geonameid, admin1-4_code, country_code) and describes model output by CF standard names (cf_standard_name); nasa-gesdisc-kg geo-locates by lat/long bounding box and tags datasets with internal-node GCMD keywords. No common identifier scheme on either the spatial or the variable axis.

known_non_joins.attempted_key: S2 Level-13 cell (re-confirm) / SAWGraph PWS reference IRI (re-confirm)
known_non_joins.diagnosis: Re-affirms the two base-file geoconnex non-joins: 0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes with spatialkg, and the sawgraph owl:sameAs -> geoconnex.us/ref/pws/ reference IRIs are not materialized as geoconnex subjects. These remain genuine non-joins; they are a SEPARATE key from the county/state FIPS that DOES join (cluster L1-L3). Use the FIPS route, not S2/PWS, to reach geoconnex from the spatial hub.

fiokg links facilities to counties (AdministrativeRegion_2) and S2 cells via owl:sameAs, but carries NO KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS2} state IRIs - all 3,031 matched admin regions are AdministrativeRegion_2 counties, 0 are AdministrativeRegion_1 states. There is therefore no direct state-level join key; roll up to state via spatialkg's own admin-region hierarchy (county -> state) after attaching at the county level (N2).

Of fiokg's 3,301 distinct administrativeRegion.USA owl:sameAs objects, 256 have a 3-digit suffix and 5 have a 4-digit suffix (e.g. .../administrativeRegion.USA.123, .../administrativeRegion.USA.6061) - county FIPS that lost one or two leading zeros, presumably via numeric handling upstream. These do NOT match spatialkg's zero-padded administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS5} nodes verbatim and are excluded from the N2 count (3,031). They are NOT a different geography - they are damaged encodings of counties already partly covered by the clean 5-digit set. A zero-pad-and-rebuild recovery is conceivable but UNVERIFIED (ambiguous: a 3-digit '123' could pad to several states) and was not pursued; the clean 5-digit county key (N2/N3) is the reliable route.

known_non_joins.attempted_key: MedGen / UMLS CUI (MONDO -> ubergraph UMLS:C -> prokn medgen seeAlso)
known_non_joins.diagnosis: prokn carries MedGen IRIs (ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/medgen/C{cui}, 7,293) on rdfs:seeAlso ONLY from its biolink EFO_0000651 'DiseaseOrPhenotype' phenotype nodes; its curated up:Disease (http://purl.uniprot.org/core/Disease) entries carry MONDO/OMIM/Orphanet/DOID seeAlso but ZERO MedGen. So a MONDO->UMLS:C bridge matches phenotype nodes (739), not disease entities (0 on up:Disease). Use the four up:Disease linkages A3 (MONDO) / A12 (OMIM) / A14 (Orphanet) / A15 (DOID); do not add a MedGen bridge.

superseded_non_joins.base_file_diagnosis: Urban Flooding OKN is large; the node-IRI / domain-predicate scan timed out even at sample=300000 and surfaced no standard crosswalk namespaces in the slice seen. No S2 / KWG admin-region / geoconnex join to the SAWGraph spatial hub was confirmable here.
superseded_non_joins.correction: SUPERSEDED. The scan timed out because it tried to enumerate node IRIs / domain predicates over ~11.7M S2 nodes unbounded. With a type-bounded inner sub-SELECT and an object-shape regex filter (avoiding the constant-predicate-on-blank-node gotcha), ufokn profiles fine and DOES carry a standard crosswalk key: S2 Level-13 cell ids in schema:value (cluster O1).

superseded_non_joins.base_file_diagnosis: Urban Flooding OKN shares 0 S2Cell_Level13 nodes with spatialkg (direct typed-node join). Despite being a spatial KG it does not attach to the SAWGraph S2 hub in the slice reachable here; it uses a different spatial encoding.
superseded_non_joins.correction: SUPERSEDED. The '0' came from a DIRECT typed-NODE join (?ufokn_node == ?spatialkg_S2Cell_Level13_node), which cannot match because ufokn's S2 cells are BLANK NODES, not the KWG resource IRIs spatialkg materializes. The 'different spatial encoding' is exactly the fix: the cell id is in schema:value as a bare decimal 64-bit integer; rebuild http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{value} and the join is direct. Three samples (1k/8k/20k cells) show 100% containment of ufokn level-13 cells in spatialkg (cluster O1).

superseded_non_joins.attempted_key: PubChem CID (oa:hasTarget / dc:subject) -> chemical cluster CAS / DTXSID
superseded_non_joins.base_file_diagnosis: biobricks-pubchem-annotations is keyed ENTIRELY on PubChem CID: both oa:hasTarget (14.4M distinct) and dc:subject objects are http://rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/{compound,substance}/CID{n} IRIs. No federation KG materializes PubChem CID - the chemical cluster (biobricks-ice/tox21/toxcast) keys on CAS + EPA DTXSID via edamontology:has_identifier (no CID predicate at all), and identifier-mappings was already confirmed CID-free (see the identifier-mappings non-join). CAS numbers appear only as unstructured free text inside synonym annotation bodies, not as a clean materialized key, so there is no reliable CID->CAS bridge to the existing CAS cluster (B/E). Island on a clean-key basis; a CAS-from-synonym-text extraction is a fragile, UNVERIFIED thin thread.
superseded_non_joins.correction: SUPERSEDED by cluster P1. The base verdict checked only the CAS chemical cluster and identifier-mappings and concluded 'no federation KG materializes PubChem CID'. It never profiled spoke-okn's oboInOwl:hasDbXref, which carries 762 http://identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{CID} ids on its biolink:ChemicalEntity nodes - i.e. spoke-okn DOES materialize PubChem CID, just in identifiers.org CURIE-IRI form rather than biobricks' rdf.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/.../CID{n} node form. After bare-CID normalization the join is exact: 762/762 of spoke-okn's CID-bearing compounds match a biobricks annotation (verified 2026-06-11). The CAS->synonym-text thread remains the only (still fragile) route to the CAS cluster, but biobricks-pubchem-annotations is no longer an island: it attaches to the chemical world through spoke-okn on PubChem CID.

islands.note: Profiled and found to share no materialized public join key for their domain entities (custom IRIs / free-text literals / identifiers with no federation partner). CORRECTIONS vs the base file: scales and nikg are NO LONGER islands - they attach to the SAWGraph spatial hub on county FIPS (cluster L4-L7); and ruralkg, dreamkg and sudokn join spoke-okn on geography (ZIP - cluster J; county FIPS - cluster K). Current islands below: maudekg and nasa-gesdisc-kg are detailed under known_non_joins; phaseskg is a pure T-Box ontology with no instance data. UPDATE (2026-06-11 profiling pass): biobricks-pubchem-annotations added as an island - it is keyed solely on PubChem CID, which no federation KG materializes (detailed under known_non_joins). Its sibling biobricks-mesh is NOT an island: it attaches to ubergraph (and thereby the disease cluster) on MeSH descriptor ids - cluster M1. UPDATE (2026-06-11 fiokg pass): fiokg is NOT an island - it joins the SAWGraph spatial hub on S2 cells + county FIPS (N1-N3, N6) and the manufacturing cluster on NAICS (N4-N5). UPDATE (2026-06-11 ufokn pass): ufokn is NOT an island and was never listed as one (it was 'unprofiled-large'); it attaches to the SAWGraph spatial hub on S2 Level-13 cells (cluster O1), so its base-file 'unprofiled-large' and 'S2 = 0' verdicts are superseded (see superseded_non_joins). UPDATE (2026-06-11 PubChem-CID pass): biobricks-pubchem-annotations is NO LONGER an island. It joins spoke-okn on PubChem CID (cluster P1): spoke-okn carries 762 identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/{CID} ids via oboInOwl:hasDbXref - missed by the original pass, which only checked the CAS cluster and identifier-mappings. Exact join 762/762 (100%); the prior 'PubChem-CID island' known_non_join is moved to superseded_non_joins.
thin_threads: ruralkg has 7 DrugBank drug IRIs (could touch rdkg DrugBank) and ~23 Wikidata links
ruralkg: censusCounty (KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}) joins spoke-okn (K2) and the spatialkg hub; postalCode joins spoke-okn ZIPs (J2).
dreamkg: schema:postalCode joins spoke-okn on 53 Philadelphia ZIPs (J3).
sudokn: hasZipcodeValue joins spoke-okn on 12,329 ZIPs (J1).

phaseskg: a small OBO-style ontology (the PHASES 'Promoting Healthy Aging through Semantic Enrichment' vocabulary, ~377 typed entities) with NO instance data. It is built on OBO upper ontologies (BFO, IAO, OMO, RO, GO, MF, MFOEM, OGMS, OBI); 23 of its imported non-PHASES OBO terms also exist (as labelled classes) in ubergraph - an ontology-scaffolding overlap on identical obo IRIs, NOT a data join. It carries no MONDO/HP/DOID/CHEBI/gene/geographic identifiers, so it connects no instance data to any other KG. Treat as an island for federated-query purposes.

wildlifekn: 5,205 reified observation Statements over 657 Location nodes, 303 Bird_name and 97 Amphibian_name nodes. Species are stored as scientific-name LABEL strings WITH taxonomic authority (e.g. 'Ardea alba Linnaeus, 1758', 'Coragyps atratus (Bechstein, 1793)') - there are NO NCBITaxon IRIs. A join to ubergraph NCBITaxon is conceivable by stripping the authority to the bare binomial (first two tokens) and matching ubergraph rdfs:label, analogous to base-file D3 but LABEL-based rather than IRI-based and therefore fragile - this was NOT verified (the endpoint became unresponsive before the count completed; UNVERIFIED). Location nodes carry ONLY rdf:type + rdfs:label (free-text place/county names, almost all in Florida, e.g. 'Sarasota County','Orange County','Saint Petersburg') with no FIPS, coordinates, or admin-region IRI, so there is no clean geographic key to the SAWGraph hub (a county-name->FIPS gazetteer would be required). UNVERIFIED.

fiokg <-> ufokn: SUPERSEDED for ufokn - ufokn DOES carry an S2 Level-13 key (in schema:value; cluster O1), so both fiokg (national, N1) and ufokn (continental US, O1) are S2 leaves on the spatialkg hub and meet through it in any shared cell; a DIRECT leaf-to-leaf fiokg<->ufokn S2 count was not separately verified (both graphs are heavy on the S2 predicate; regional sampling makes a direct count unreliable - assert via the hub). The remaining KGs in this thread (nasa-gesdisc-kg / wildlifekn / climatemodelskg) still carry no KWG S2/admin-region key fiokg can meet (per their known_non_joins); fiokg has no confirmed direct spatial join to them. UNVERIFIED for fiokg<->those specifically.

fiokg EnvironmentalInterest / program-type classes (AirProgram, HazardousWasteProgram, ChemicalReleaseProgram, etc.) and the EPA-PFAS-Facility flag are fiokg-internal controlled vocabularies with no off-the-shelf cross-KG identifier; they are useful internal filters (e.g. restrict N6 to EPA-PFAS-Facility) but not join keys to another graph.
